items.briefing:
Australian financial services organisations are being warned they face the highest rate of cyberattacks per device of any sector, making strong security practices especially critical for staff in banking, insurance, and related industries. Separately, OpenAI has moved to require hardware-backed passkeys for access to its systems — a sign that traditional passwords are increasingly considered insufficient protection across the tech industry.

Scam activity continues to be monitored through Scamwatch, so staff should remain alert to suspicious emails, calls, or messages. **Practical tip:** If you work in financial services, ensure your work accounts use multi-factor authentication — contact your IT team if you're unsure whether it's enabled.

featured.title: Financial services faces most cyberattacks per device
featured.quote: Australian banks face critical security risks from outdated systems and delayed security patches.
